mainMenu.title: GasTown
mainMenu.subtitle:
A game about surviving Auschwitz-Birkenau
May 15, 1944 — January 27, 1945
mainMenu.contentWarning:
This game depicts the Holocaust with historical accuracy.
It includes descriptions of atrocity, forced complicity,
and the moral cost of survival.

It is intended as interactive remembrance, not entertainment.

stats.staminaDesc: Physical capacity to continue. Restored by rations and rest.
stats.usefulnessDesc: Your value to your captors. Below 15, you risk selection.
stats.dissociationDesc: Psychological distance from your actions. Hidden from you during play.
stats.rationsDesc: Daily food allocation. More than most prisoners. Less than a human being requires.

sonderkommando.title: Sonderkommando
sonderkommando.subtitle: May 15 — August 12, 1944
sonderkommando.description: Jewish prisoners assigned to the crematoria. A special kommando. A special hell.

medical.title: Medical Assistant
medical.subtitle: August 12 — December 2, 1944
medical.description: Dr. Mengele's pathologist. Your expertise is your protection. Your protection is your prison.

pathologist.title: Pathologist and Witness
pathologist.subtitle: December 2, 1944 — January 27, 1945
pathologist.description: You have survived long enough to become a witness. This is both more and less than it sounds.

liberation_witness.title: Liberation: The Witness
liberation_witness.text:
January 27, 1945. You hear Soviet voices.

You survived. Your Dissociation stayed low — you remained present for the horror, resisted where you could, documented everything.

You wrote your testimony. It was published. The world read it.

You spent forty years carrying what you had seen, and forty years making sure others would not look away.

Dr. Miklós Nyiszli died in 1956. His book was published in 1946.
His testimony is why we know what we know.

liberation_empty.title: Liberation: The Empty
liberation_empty.text:
January 27, 1945. You hear Soviet voices.

You survived. Your Dissociation was high — you learned to function without feeling. You were efficient. You endured.

But the self that survived is not the same self that arrived.

You struggle to write testimony. The words feel like they belong to someone else. Someone you buried somewhere between the spring and the winter.

Survival was the goal. You achieved it.
The cost is still being calculated.

liberation_fighter.title: Liberation: The One Who Fought
liberation_fighter.text:
January 27, 1945. You hear Soviet voices.

You fought on October 7th. You resisted where you could. Your Dissociation is low — you stayed human at enormous cost.

You barely survived. There were many days when you almost didn't.

But when you write your testimony, the words come easily.
You know what you did. You know what you didn't do.
The ledger is not clean — no ledger from that place can be clean.
But it is yours, and you can account for every line.

This, perhaps, is the most you could have hoped for.

death_selection.title: Selected
death_selection.text:
Your usefulness dropped below the threshold that sustained you.

You were selected.

This happened to most of the Sonderkommando.
The three-month rotation was policy, not exception.

Of approximately 2,200 Sonderkommando who served in
Auschwitz-Birkenau, approximately 110 survived.

Their names are partially known.
The names of those who did not survive are mostly lost.

Remember this when you wonder whether any individual choice
in an impossible system could have mattered.

death_starvation.title: Exhaustion
death_starvation.text:
Your body gave out.

Even the Sonderkommando, who ate better than most prisoners,
could not sustain the physical demands of their forced labor indefinitely.

Your body was a tool that was used until it broke.

This was policy.

deathWarnings: Your usefulness is dangerously low. The next selection may find you.
Stamina critical. You cannot work. Without work, you have no value.
The SS have noticed your declining output.

dayMessages.text: You have survived your first day.
One week. You no longer count hours.
The revolt has happened. The camp is changed.
You can hear the guns to the east.
